name: Penicillins
description: Penicillin-class beta-lactam antibiotics; includes amoxicillin and related aminopenicillins.
clinical_note: Use for documented penicillin allergy, especially immediate hypersensitivity, anaphylaxis, or severe cutaneous adverse reactions.
source: DailyMed/OpenFDA label evidence + CDC penicillin allergy guidance

name: Beta-lactams
description: Broad beta-lactam antibiotic family including penicillins, cephalosporins, carbapenems, and monobactams.
clinical_note: Parent review group only; do not treat every beta-lactam allergy as equivalent without reaction history and drug side-chain review.

name: Cephalosporins
clinical_note: Cross-reactivity with penicillin allergy is possible but varies by generation, side chain, and reaction history; third-generation risk is low in many settings.

name: Carbapenems
clinical_note: Use as a beta-lactam review group; CDC notes later-generation cephalosporins and carbapenems can be safe in selected patients without recent IgE-mediated penicillin symptoms.

name: Sulfonamide antibiotics
description: Antibacterial sulfonamides, such as sulfamethoxazole-containing medicines.
clinical_note: Keep distinct from non-antibiotic sulfonamides; AAAAI states clinically significant immune cross-reactivity with non-antibiotic sulfonamides is not supported.

name: NSAIDs
description: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs; includes ibuprofen, diclofenac, and naproxen.
clinical_note: For suspected allergy to a non-selective NSAID, NICE advises future avoidance of all non-selective NSAIDs and specialist referral after severe reactions.

name: Salicylates
clinical_note: Aspirin/salicylate hypersensitivity can cross-react clinically with non-selective NSAIDs, especially COX-1 mediated respiratory or urticaria/angioedema phenotypes.

name: Anticoagulants
description: Anticoagulant medicines in the priority set; includes warfarin and heparin.
clinical_note: Use for documented hypersensitivity to anticoagulants; distinguish allergy from bleeding risk or expected pharmacologic effect.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + DailyMed/OpenFDA label evidence

name: Heparins
clinical_note: Use for documented heparin allergy or heparin-induced thrombocytopenia history; HIT is an immune adverse reaction and should be handled distinctly from simple intolerance.

name: Antiplatelets
description: Antiplatelet medicines in the priority set; includes aspirin and clopidogrel.
clinical_note: Use for documented hypersensitivity to antiplatelet medicines; distinguish allergy from bleeding risk.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + DailyMed/OpenFDA label evidence

name: Biguanides
clinical_note: Use for documented metformin hypersensitivity; distinguish allergy from gastrointestinal intolerance or lactic acidosis risk.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + RxNorm identity

name: Sulfonylureas
clinical_note: Different from sulfonamide antibiotic allergy; keep distinct to avoid false-positive sulfa alerts.

name: Insulins
description: Insulin preparations and analogues; includes insulin glargine.
clinical_note: Can involve insulin molecule or excipients; reaction details matter.

name: ACE inhibitors
description: Angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors; includes enalapril and ramipril.
clinical_note: ACE inhibitor angioedema is clinically important and should trigger same-class avoidance/review; distinguish from cough.
source: DailyMed/OpenFDA label evidence + NICE drug-allergy documentation

name: Calcium channel blockers
clinical_note: Use for documented hypersensitivity-type reactions; distinguish from edema, flushing, or hypotension.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + RxNorm identity

name: Loop diuretics
clinical_note: Use for documented loop-diuretic hypersensitivity; do not equate with sulfonamide antibiotic allergy without evidence.
source: AAAAI sulfonamide cross-reactivity guidance + RxNorm identity

name: Potassium-sparing diuretics
clinical_note: Use for documented hypersensitivity-type reactions; distinguish from endocrine side effects or hyperkalemia risk.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + RxNorm identity

name: Cardiac glycosides
clinical_note: Use for documented digoxin hypersensitivity; distinguish from toxicity.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + RxNorm identity

name: Statins
description: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors; includes atorvastatin and simvastatin.
clinical_note: Distinguish allergy from intolerance such as myalgia; alert only for documented hypersensitivity-type reactions.

name: Corticosteroids
clinical_note: Use for documented corticosteroid hypersensitivity or excipient allergy; distinguish from predictable steroid adverse effects.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + RxNorm identity

name: Fluoroquinolones
clinical_note: Avoid same-class rechallenge after severe immediate hypersensitivity unless specialist-supervised.

name: Nitroimidazoles
clinical_note: Use for documented metronidazole/nitroimidazole hypersensitivity.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + RxNorm identity

name: Macrolides
clinical_note: Allergy is less common but same-class alternatives require review when reaction was severe.

name: Azole antifungals
clinical_note: Use for documented azole antifungal hypersensitivity; distinguish from hepatic or QT risk.
source: NICE Drug allergy CG183 + RxNorm identity

name: Anticonvulsants
description: Antiseizure medicines; includes carbamazepine and valproate for this priority-drug seed set.
clinical_note: Severe cutaneous adverse reactions should be treated as high-risk drug allergy history and referred/reviewed.

name: SSRIs
description: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors; includes fluoxetine and sertraline.
clinical_note: True allergy is uncommon; document rash, angioedema, or severe reactions separately from side effects.

name: PPIs
clinical_note: Class membership for documented PPI hypersensitivity; true allergy should be separated from intolerance.

name: Opioids
description: Opioid analgesics and opioid-like medicines; includes tramadol.
clinical_note: Differentiate true allergy from histamine-mediated itching, nausea, or other intolerance.
